speaker: OTHELLO
text: Good Michael, look you to the guard tonight.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let’s teach ourselves that honorable stop

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Not to outsport discretion.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Iago hath direction what to do,

speaker: CASSIO
text: But notwithstanding, with my personal eye

speaker: CASSIO
text: Will I look to ’t.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Iago is most honest.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Michael, goodnight. Tomorrow with your earliest

speaker: OTHELLO
text:
Let me have speech with you.
                 
                To
                 
                Desdemona
                .
               Come, my dear love,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The purchase made, the fruits are to ensue;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That profit’s yet to come ’tween me and you.—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Goodnight.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Welcome, Iago. We must to the watch.

speaker: IAGO
text: Not this hour, lieutenant. ’Tis not yet ten o’ th’

speaker: IAGO
text: clock. Our general cast us thus early for the love of

speaker: IAGO
text: his Desdemona—who let us not therefore blame;

speaker: IAGO
text: he hath not yet made wanton the night with her, and

speaker: IAGO
text: she is sport for Jove.

speaker: CASSIO
text: She’s a most exquisite lady.

speaker: IAGO
text: And, I’ll warrant her, full of game.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Indeed, she’s a most fresh and delicate

speaker: CASSIO
text: creature.

speaker: IAGO
text: What an eye she has! Methinks it sounds a parley

speaker: IAGO
text: to provocation.

speaker: CASSIO
text: An inviting eye, and yet methinks right

speaker: CASSIO
text: modest.

speaker: IAGO
text: And when she speaks, is it not an alarum to love?

speaker: CASSIO
text: She is indeed perfection.

speaker: IAGO
text: Well, happiness to their sheets! Come, lieutenant,

speaker: IAGO
text: I have a stoup of wine; and here without are a

speaker: IAGO
text: brace of Cyprus gallants that would fain have a

speaker: IAGO
text: measure to the health of black Othello.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Not tonight, good Iago. I have very poor and

speaker: CASSIO
text: unhappy brains for drinking. I could well wish

speaker: CASSIO
text: courtesy would invent some other custom of

speaker: CASSIO
text: entertainment.

speaker: IAGO
text: O, they are our friends! But one cup; I’ll drink

speaker: IAGO
text: for you.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I have drunk but one cup tonight, and that was

speaker: CASSIO
text: craftily qualified too, and behold what innovation it

speaker: CASSIO
text: makes here. I am unfortunate in the infirmity and

speaker: CASSIO
text: dare not task my weakness with any more.

speaker: IAGO
text: What, man! ’Tis a night of revels. The gallants

speaker: IAGO
text: desire it.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Where are they?

speaker: IAGO
text: Here at the door. I pray you, call them in.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I’ll do ’t, but it dislikes me.

speaker: IAGO
text: If I can fasten but one cup upon him

speaker: IAGO
text: With that which he hath drunk tonight already,

speaker: IAGO
text: He’ll be as full of quarrel and offense

speaker: IAGO
text: As my young mistress’ dog. Now my sick fool Roderigo,

speaker: IAGO
text: Whom love hath turned almost the wrong side out,

speaker: IAGO
text: To Desdemona hath tonight caroused

speaker: IAGO
text: Potations pottle-deep; and he’s to watch.

speaker: IAGO
text: Three else of Cyprus, noble swelling spirits

speaker: IAGO
text: That hold their honors in a wary distance,

speaker: IAGO
text: The very elements of this warlike isle,

speaker: IAGO
text: Have I tonight flustered with flowing cups;

speaker: IAGO
text: And they watch too. Now, ’mongst this flock of drunkards

speaker: IAGO
text: Am I to put our Cassio in some action

speaker: IAGO
text: That may offend the isle. But here they come.

speaker: IAGO
text: If consequence do but approve my dream,

speaker: IAGO
text: My boat sails freely both with wind and stream.

speaker: CASSIO
text: ’Fore God, they have given me a rouse

speaker: CASSIO
text: already.

speaker: MONTANO
text: Good faith, a little one; not past a pint, as I

speaker: MONTANO
text: am a soldier.

speaker: IAGO
text: Some wine, ho!

speaker: IAGO
text: And let me the cannikin clink, clink,

speaker: IAGO
text: And let me the cannikin clink.

speaker: IAGO
text: A soldier’s a man,

speaker: IAGO
text: O, man’s life’s but a span,

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, then, let a soldier drink.

speaker: IAGO
text: Some wine, boys!

speaker: CASSIO
text: ’Fore God, an excellent song.

speaker: IAGO
text: I learned it in England, where indeed they are

speaker: IAGO
text: most potent in potting. Your Dane, your German,

speaker: IAGO
text: and your swag-bellied Hollander—drink, ho!—are

speaker: IAGO
text: nothing to your English.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Is your Englishman so exquisite in his

speaker: CASSIO
text: drinking?

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, he drinks you, with facility, your Dane

speaker: IAGO
text: dead drunk. He sweats not to overthrow your Almain.

speaker: IAGO
text: He gives your Hollander a vomit ere the next

speaker: IAGO
text: pottle can be filled.

speaker: CASSIO
text: To the health of our general!

speaker: MONTANO
text: I am for it, lieutenant, and I’ll do you

speaker: MONTANO
text: justice.

speaker: IAGO
text: O sweet England!

speaker: IAGO
text: King Stephen was and-a worthy peer,

speaker: IAGO
text: His breeches cost him but a crown;

speaker: IAGO
text: He held them sixpence all too dear;

speaker: IAGO
text: With that he called the tailor lown.

speaker: IAGO
text: He was a wight of high renown,

speaker: IAGO
text: And thou art but of low degree;

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Tis pride that pulls the country down,

speaker: IAGO
text: Then take thy auld cloak about thee.

speaker: IAGO
text: Some wine, ho!

speaker: CASSIO
text: ’Fore God, this is a more exquisite song than

speaker: CASSIO
text: the other!

speaker: IAGO
text: Will you hear ’t again?

speaker: CASSIO
text: No, for I hold him to be unworthy of his place

speaker: CASSIO
text: that does those things. Well, God’s above all; and

speaker: CASSIO
text: there be souls must be saved, and there be souls

speaker: CASSIO
text: must not be saved.

speaker: IAGO
text: It’s true, good lieutenant.

speaker: CASSIO
text: For mine own part—no offense to the General,

speaker: CASSIO
text: nor any man of quality—I hope to be saved.

speaker: IAGO
text: And so do I too, lieutenant.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Ay, but, by your leave, not before me. The

speaker: CASSIO
text: Lieutenant is to be saved before the Ancient. Let’s

speaker: CASSIO
text: have no more of this. Let’s to our affairs. God

speaker: CASSIO
text: forgive us our sins! Gentlemen, let’s look to our

speaker: CASSIO
text: business. Do not think, gentlemen, I am drunk. This

speaker: CASSIO
text: is my ancient, this is my right hand, and this is my

speaker: CASSIO
text: left. I am not drunk now. I can stand well enough,

speaker: CASSIO
text: and I speak well enough.

speaker: GENTLEMEN
text: Excellent well.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Why, very well then. You must not think then

speaker: CASSIO
text: that I am drunk.

speaker: MONTANO
text: To th’ platform, masters. Come, let’s set the watch.

speaker: IAGO
text: You see this fellow that is gone before?

speaker: IAGO
text: He’s a soldier fit to stand by Caesar

speaker: IAGO
text: And give direction; and do but see his vice.

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Tis to his virtue a just equinox,

speaker: IAGO
text: The one as long as th’ other. ’Tis pity of him.

speaker: IAGO
text: I fear the trust Othello puts him in,

speaker: IAGO
text: On some odd time of his infirmity,

speaker: IAGO
text: Will shake this island.

speaker: MONTANO
text: But is he often thus?

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Tis evermore the prologue to his sleep.

speaker: IAGO
text: He’ll watch the horologe a double set

speaker: IAGO
text: If drink rock not his cradle.

speaker: MONTANO
text: It were well

speaker: MONTANO
text: The General were put in mind of it.

speaker: MONTANO
text: Perhaps he sees it not, or his good nature

speaker: MONTANO
text: Prizes the virtue that appears in Cassio

speaker: MONTANO
text: And looks not on his evils. Is not this true?

speaker: IAGO
text: How now, Roderigo?

speaker: IAGO
text: I pray you, after the Lieutenant, go.

speaker: MONTANO
text: And ’tis great pity that the noble Moor

speaker: MONTANO
text: Should hazard such a place as his own second

speaker: MONTANO
text: With one of an engraffed infirmity.

speaker: MONTANO
text: It were an honest action to say so

speaker: MONTANO
text: To the Moor.

speaker: IAGO
text: Not I, for this fair island.

speaker: IAGO
text: I do love Cassio well and would do much

speaker: IAGO
text: To cure him of this evil—

speaker: IAGO
text: But hark! What noise?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Zounds, you rogue, you rascal!

speaker: MONTANO
text: What’s the matter, lieutenant?

speaker: CASSIO
text: A knave teach me my duty? I’ll beat the knave

speaker: CASSIO
text: into a twiggen bottle.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Beat me?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Dost thou prate, rogue?

speaker: MONTANO
text: Nay, good lieutenant. I pray you, sir, hold

speaker: MONTANO
text: your hand.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Let me go, sir, or I’ll knock you o’er the

speaker: CASSIO
text: mazard.

speaker: MONTANO
text: Come, come, you’re drunk.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Drunk?

speaker: IAGO
text: Away, I say! Go out and cry a mutiny.

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, good lieutenant.—God’s will, gentlemen!—

speaker: IAGO
text: Help, ho! Lieutenant—sir—Montano—sir—

speaker: IAGO
text: Help, masters!—Here’s a goodly watch indeed!

speaker: IAGO
text: Who’s that which rings the bell? Diablo, ho!

speaker: IAGO
text: The town will rise. God’s will, lieutenant, hold!

speaker: IAGO
text: You will be shamed forever.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What is the matter here?

speaker: MONTANO
text: Zounds, I bleed still.

speaker: MONTANO
text: I am hurt to th’ death. He dies!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Hold, for your lives!

speaker: IAGO
text: Hold, ho! Lieutenant—sir—Montano— gentlemen—

speaker: IAGO
text: Have you forgot all sense of place and duty?

speaker: IAGO
text: Hold! The General speaks to you. Hold, for shame!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why, how now, ho! From whence ariseth this?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Are we turned Turks, and to ourselves do that

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Which heaven hath forbid the Ottomites?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For Christian shame, put by this barbarous brawl!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: He that stirs next to carve for his own rage

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Holds his soul light; he dies upon his motion.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Silence that dreadful bell. It frights the isle

speaker: OTHELLO
text: From her propriety. What is the matter, masters?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Honest Iago, that looks dead with grieving,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Speak. Who began this? On thy love, I charge thee.

speaker: IAGO
text: I do not know. Friends all but now, even now,

speaker: IAGO
text: In quarter and in terms like bride and groom

speaker: IAGO
text: Divesting them for bed; and then but now,

speaker: IAGO
text: As if some planet had unwitted men,

speaker: IAGO
text: Swords out, and tilting one at other’s breast,

speaker: IAGO
text: In opposition bloody. I cannot speak

speaker: IAGO
text: Any beginning to this peevish odds,

speaker: IAGO
text: And would in action glorious I had lost

speaker: IAGO
text: Those legs that brought me to a part of it!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: How comes it, Michael, you are thus forgot?

speaker: CASSIO
text: I pray you pardon me; I cannot speak.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Worthy Montano, you were wont be civil.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The gravity and stillness of your youth

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The world hath noted. And your name is great

speaker: OTHELLO
text: In mouths of wisest censure. What’s the matter

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That you unlace your reputation thus,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And spend your rich opinion for the name

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of a night-brawler? Give me answer to it.

speaker: MONTANO
text: Worthy Othello, I am hurt to danger.

speaker: MONTANO
text: Your officer Iago can inform you,

speaker: MONTANO
text: While I spare speech, which something now offends me,

speaker: MONTANO
text: Of all that I do know; nor know I aught

speaker: MONTANO
text: By me that’s said or done amiss this night,

speaker: MONTANO
text: Unless self-charity be sometimes a vice,

speaker: MONTANO
text: And to defend ourselves it be a sin

speaker: MONTANO
text: When violence assails us.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Now, by heaven,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: My blood begins my safer guides to rule,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And passion, having my best judgment collied,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Assays to lead the way. Zounds, if I stir,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Or do but lift this arm, the best of you

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Shall sink in my rebuke. Give me to know

speaker: OTHELLO
text: How this foul rout began, who set it on;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And he that is approved in this offense,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Though he had twinned with me, both at a birth,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Shall lose me. What, in a town of war

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yet wild, the people’s hearts brimful of fear,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To manage private and domestic quarrel,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: In night, and on the court and guard of safety?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis monstrous. Iago, who began ’t?

speaker: MONTANO
text: If partially affined, or leagued in office,

speaker: MONTANO
text: Thou dost deliver more or less than truth,

speaker: MONTANO
text: Thou art no soldier.

speaker: IAGO
text: Touch me not so near.

speaker: IAGO
text: I had rather have this tongue cut from my mouth

speaker: IAGO
text: Than it should do offense to Michael Cassio.

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet I persuade myself, to speak the truth

speaker: IAGO
text: Shall nothing wrong him. Thus it is, general:

speaker: IAGO
text: Montano and myself being in speech,

speaker: IAGO
text: There comes a fellow crying out for help,

speaker: IAGO
text: And Cassio following him with determined sword

speaker: IAGO
text: To execute upon him. Sir, this gentleman

speaker: IAGO
text: Steps in to Cassio and entreats his pause.

speaker: IAGO
text: Myself the crying fellow did pursue,

speaker: IAGO
text: Lest by his clamor—as it so fell out—

speaker: IAGO
text: The town might fall in fright. He, swift of foot,

speaker: IAGO
text: Outran my purpose, and I returned the rather

speaker: IAGO
text: For that I heard the clink and fall of swords

speaker: IAGO
text: And Cassio high in oath, which till tonight

speaker: IAGO
text: I ne’er might say before. When I came back—

speaker: IAGO
text: For this was brief—I found them close together

speaker: IAGO
text: At blow and thrust, even as again they were

speaker: IAGO
text: When you yourself did part them.

speaker: IAGO
text: More of this matter cannot I report.

speaker: IAGO
text: But men are men; the best sometimes forget.

speaker: IAGO
text: Though Cassio did some little wrong to him,

speaker: IAGO
text: As men in rage strike those that wish them best,

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet surely Cassio, I believe, received

speaker: IAGO
text: From him that fled some strange indignity

speaker: IAGO
text: Which patience could not pass.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I know, Iago,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thy honesty and love doth mince this matter,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Making it light to Cassio.—Cassio, I love thee,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But nevermore be officer of mine.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Look if my gentle love be not raised up!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll make thee an example.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What is the matter, dear?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: All’s well now, sweeting.

speaker: OTHELLO
text:
Come away to bed.
                 
                To
                 
                Montano
                .
               Sir, for your hurts,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Myself will be your surgeon.—Lead him off.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Iago, look with care about the town

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And silence those whom this vile brawl distracted.—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Come, Desdemona. ’Tis the soldier’s life

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To have their balmy slumbers waked with strife.

speaker: IAGO
text: What, are you hurt, lieutenant?

speaker: CASSIO
text: Ay, past all surgery.

speaker: IAGO
text: Marry, God forbid!

speaker: CASSIO
text: Reputation, reputation, reputation! O, I have

speaker: CASSIO
text: lost my reputation! I have lost the immortal part of

speaker: CASSIO
text: myself, and what remains is bestial. My reputation,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Iago, my reputation!

speaker: IAGO
text: As I am an honest man, I thought you had

speaker: IAGO
text: received some bodily wound. There is more sense

speaker: IAGO
text: in that than in reputation. Reputation is an idle and

speaker: IAGO
text: most false imposition, oft got without merit and lost

speaker: IAGO
text: without deserving. You have lost no reputation at

speaker: IAGO
text: all, unless you repute yourself such a loser. What,

speaker: IAGO
text: man, there are ways to recover the General again!

speaker: IAGO
text: You are but now cast in his mood—a punishment

speaker: IAGO
text: more in policy than in malice, even so as one would

speaker: IAGO
text: beat his offenseless dog to affright an imperious

speaker: IAGO
text: lion. Sue to him again and he’s yours.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I will rather sue to be despised than to deceive

speaker: CASSIO
text: so good a commander with so slight, so drunken,

speaker: CASSIO
text: and so indiscreet an officer. Drunk? And speak

speaker: CASSIO
text: parrot? And squabble? Swagger? Swear? And discourse

speaker: CASSIO
text: fustian with one’s own shadow? O thou

speaker: CASSIO
text: invisible spirit of wine, if thou hast no name to be

speaker: CASSIO
text: known by, let us call thee devil!

speaker: IAGO
text: What was he that you followed with your sword?

speaker: IAGO
text: What had he done to you?

speaker: CASSIO
text: I know not.

speaker: IAGO
text: Is ’t possible?

speaker: CASSIO
text: I remember a mass of things, but nothing

speaker: CASSIO
text: distinctly; a quarrel, but nothing wherefore. O

speaker: CASSIO
text: God, that men should put an enemy in their

speaker: CASSIO
text: mouths to steal away their brains! That we should

speaker: CASSIO
text: with joy, pleasance, revel, and applause transform

speaker: CASSIO
text: ourselves into beasts!

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, but you are now well enough. How came

speaker: IAGO
text: you thus recovered?

speaker: CASSIO
text: It hath pleased the devil drunkenness to give

speaker: CASSIO
text: place to the devil wrath. One unperfectness shows

speaker: CASSIO
text: me another, to make me frankly despise myself.

speaker: IAGO
text: Come, you are too severe a moraler. As the time,

speaker: IAGO
text: the place, and the condition of this country stands,

speaker: IAGO
text: I could heartily wish this had not so befallen. But

speaker: IAGO
text: since it is as it is, mend it for your own good.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I will ask him for my place again; he shall tell

speaker: CASSIO
text: me I am a drunkard! Had I as many mouths as

speaker: CASSIO
text: Hydra, such an answer would stop them all. To be

speaker: CASSIO
text: now a sensible man, by and by a fool, and presently

speaker: CASSIO
text: a beast! O, strange! Every inordinate cup is unblessed,

speaker: CASSIO
text: and the ingredient is a devil.

speaker: IAGO
text: Come, come, good wine is a good familiar creature,

speaker: IAGO
text: if it be well used. Exclaim no more against it.

speaker: IAGO
text: And, good lieutenant, I think you think I love you.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I have well approved it, sir.—I drunk!

speaker: IAGO
text: You or any man living may be drunk at a time,

speaker: IAGO
text: man. I’ll tell you what you shall do. Our general’s

speaker: IAGO
text: wife is now the general: I may say so in this

speaker: IAGO
text: respect, for that he hath devoted and given up

speaker: IAGO
text: himself to the contemplation, mark, and denotement

speaker: IAGO
text: of her parts and graces. Confess yourself

speaker: IAGO
text: freely to her. Importune her help to put you in your

speaker: IAGO
text: place again. She is of so free, so kind, so apt, so

speaker: IAGO
text: blessed a disposition she holds it a vice in her

speaker: IAGO
text: goodness not to do more than she is requested. This

speaker: IAGO
text: broken joint between you and her husband entreat

speaker: IAGO
text: her to splinter, and, my fortunes against any lay

speaker: IAGO
text: worth naming, this crack of your love shall grow

speaker: IAGO
text: stronger than it was before.

speaker: CASSIO
text: You advise me well.

speaker: IAGO
text: I protest, in the sincerity of love and honest

speaker: IAGO
text: kindness.

speaker: CASSIO
text: I think it freely; and betimes in the morning I

speaker: CASSIO
text: will beseech the virtuous Desdemona to undertake

speaker: CASSIO
text: for me. I am desperate of my fortunes if they check

speaker: CASSIO
text: me here.

speaker: IAGO
text: You are in the right. Good night, lieutenant. I

speaker: IAGO
text: must to the watch.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Good night, honest Iago.

speaker: IAGO
text: And what’s he, then, that says I play the villain,

speaker: IAGO
text: When this advice is free I give and honest,

speaker: IAGO
text: Probal to thinking, and indeed the course

speaker: IAGO
text: To win the Moor again? For ’tis most easy

speaker: IAGO
text: Th’ inclining Desdemona to subdue

speaker: IAGO
text: In any honest suit. She’s framed as fruitful

speaker: IAGO
text: As the free elements. And then for her

speaker: IAGO
text: To win the Moor—were ’t to renounce his baptism,

speaker: IAGO
text: All seals and symbols of redeemèd sin—

speaker: IAGO
text: His soul is so enfettered to her love

speaker: IAGO
text: That she may make, unmake, do what she list,

speaker: IAGO
text: Even as her appetite shall play the god

speaker: IAGO
text: With his weak function. How am I then a villain

speaker: IAGO
text: To counsel Cassio to this parallel course

speaker: IAGO
text: Directly to his good? Divinity of hell!

speaker: IAGO
text: When devils will the blackest sins put on,

speaker: IAGO
text: They do suggest at first with heavenly shows,

speaker: IAGO
text: As I do now. For whiles this honest fool

speaker: IAGO
text: Plies Desdemona to repair his fortune,

speaker: IAGO
text: And she for him pleads strongly to the Moor,

speaker: IAGO
text: I’ll pour this pestilence into his ear:

speaker: IAGO
text: That she repeals him for her body’s lust;

speaker: IAGO
text: And by how much she strives to do him good,

speaker: IAGO
text: She shall undo her credit with the Moor.

speaker: IAGO
text: So will I turn her virtue into pitch,

speaker: IAGO
text: And out of her own goodness make the net

speaker: IAGO
text: That shall enmesh them all.

speaker: IAGO
text: How now, Roderigo?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I do follow here in the chase, not like a

speaker: RODERIGO
text: hound that hunts, but one that fills up the cry. My

speaker: RODERIGO
text: money is almost spent, I have been tonight exceedingly

speaker: RODERIGO
text: well cudgeled, and I think the issue will be I

speaker: RODERIGO
text: shall have so much experience for my pains, and so,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: with no money at all and a little more wit, return

speaker: RODERIGO
text: again to Venice.

speaker: IAGO
text: How poor are they that have not patience!

speaker: IAGO
text: What wound did ever heal but by degrees?

speaker: IAGO
text: Thou know’st we work by wit and not by witchcraft,

speaker: IAGO
text: And wit depends on dilatory time.

speaker: IAGO
text: Does 't not go well? Cassio hath beaten thee,

speaker: IAGO
text: And thou, by that small hurt, hast cashiered Cassio.

speaker: IAGO
text: Though other things grow fair against the sun,

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet fruits that blossom first will first be ripe.

speaker: IAGO
text: Content thyself awhile. By th’ Mass, ’tis morning!

speaker: IAGO
text: Pleasure and action make the hours seem short.

speaker: IAGO
text: Retire thee; go where thou art billeted.

speaker: IAGO
text: Away, I say! Thou shalt know more hereafter.

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, get thee gone.

speaker: IAGO
text: Two things are to be done.

speaker: IAGO
text: My wife must move for Cassio to her mistress.

speaker: IAGO
text: I’ll set her on.

speaker: IAGO
text: Myself the while to draw the Moor apart

speaker: IAGO
text: And bring him jump when he may Cassio find

speaker: IAGO
text: Soliciting his wife. Ay, that’s the way.

speaker: IAGO
text: Dull not device by coldness and delay.
